item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

item.title: Green Issaquah: Restoration Event at Park Pointe
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: SE Evans St, Issaquah, WA

Join the Issaquah Alps Trails Club and Green Issaquah on the third Saturday of each month for invasive species removal events at Park Pointe! We will be removing invasive species such as Himalayan blackberry bushes to prepare for our fall planting event. These invasive plants inhibit the growth of native plants, and removing them will ensure the longevity and health of our newest additions. This event is part of our ongoing efforts to help meet the Issaquah Climate Action Plan goal of 55% tree canopy cover by 2035. When and Where Date & Time: Saturday, April 18th, 2025, 9 AM - Noon Where: Park Pointe Where to Park Parking for Park Pointe is available along SE Evans Street, which is overflow for Issaquah High School and the parking for the Sportsman Club. Where to Meet We will be meeting at the end of the dirt road at the trailhead. What to Bring Bring your own gloves if you have them. There will be gloves available for those who don't have their own. Please dress in layers and in clothing you don't mind getting dirty. Closed-toed shoes are required. Long sleeves and pants, especially denim, are strongly recommended. Bring a full water bottle and snacks. Don't forget to bring your sunscreen! Point of Contact Anne Newcomb \(anne@annenewcomb.net\) and Matt LaBelle \(mattlabelle@hotmail.com\), your work party leaders.

item.title: Homebuyer Education Workshop [VIRTUAL]
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 10 AM

This is a free five-hour workshop to help you meet the WA State Housing Finance Commission’s education requirements for down payment assistance and a variety of affordable housing programs and opportunities for qualified applicants! These requirements include the successful completion of a Homebuyers Education Seminar meeting the current FHA, Fannie Mae, FreddieMac, and SMBA benchmark standards.  At the conclusion of the workshop, participants will receive a certificate of achievement from the WA State Housing Finance Commission and DFI valid for two years from the date of issuance.  To join our next workshop, please complete and submit the following registration form at least 24-hours before your selected workshop date. Once this form has been submitted, be sure to check your email for a digital confirmation, as well as additional follow up and important information from a ULMS Housing Department staff member.   Virtual workshops are held every 3rd and 4th Saturday of the month from 10:00AM – 3:30PM. A Zoom link will be sent to you via a ULMS Housing department staff member upon completing registration.

item.title: Living with Conviction: Sentenced to Debt for Life in Washington State Exhibit
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 10 AM

Saturday, April 18, 2026, 10am - 6pm This exhibitconfronts how Washington courts have been sentencing people not only to prison but also to a lifetime of debt. Location Link: Central Library Room Location: Level 8 - Gallery Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: "Living with Conviction: Sentenced to Debt for Life in Washington State" confronts how Washington courts have been sentencing people not only to prison but also to a lifetime of debt. The project leverages multimedia storytelling and community conversations to raise awareness about and advocate for an end to crippling court-imposed costs, fees, fines, and restitution, commonly referred to as Legal Financial Obligations, or LFOs. "Living with Conviction: Sentenced to Debt for Life in Washington State," goes beyond polarizing headlines and statistics, shining a light on this injustice by sharing stories of our common humanity., Thanks to our partner Living with Conviction ., Thanks to our sponsor The Seattle Public Library Foundation . Event Logistics: The gallery is open during Central Libray's open hours., Living with Conviction is a 501\(c\)\(3\) nonprofit that works to secure economic and racial justice with and by marginalized communities, especially formerly incarcerated, through community-driven research, storytelling, and legal empowerment strategies., This exhibit runs through July 26, 2026, Learn more about Exhibits . item.title: Eric-Paul Riege: ojo|-|ólǫ́
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Henry Art Gallery, 15th Ave NE & 41st St, Seattle, WA

ojo|-|ólǫ́ is an exhibition of recent and newly commissioned work by Diné artist Eric-Paul Riege \(b. 1994, Na’nízhoozhí \[Gallup, New Mexico\]\) that includes sculpture, textile, collage, and video, activated by moments of performance. Across this work, Riege combines customary Diné practices of weaving, silversmithing, and beading with contemporary cultural forms, exploring Diné cosmology, the history of Euro-American trading posts in and adjacent to the Navajo Nation, and the notion of “authenticity” as a value marker of Indigenous art and craft. Developed in partnership between the Henry and The Bell Gallery at Brown University, ojo|-|ólǫ́ emerged from Riege’s material research with Navajo collections housed at Brown’s Haffenreffer Museum of Anthropology and the University of Washington’s Burke Museum of Natural History and Culture. The resulting body of work celebrates the ancestral knowledge and labor contained within Indigenous-made objects, while investigating the role of museums and other institutions in the dissemination and dispossession of knowledge about Indigenous cultures. For his tactile and modular soft sculptures, Riege employs exaggerated scale and combines synthetic and natural materials to playfully question ideas of authenticity and Indigenous artistic production. Processes of making and remaking embody a living practice of exchange that draws together webs of connection across time and space. The exhibition also includes objects from the Burke collection displayed alongside assorted ephemera from Riege’s own home and studio. The resulting non-hierarchal display blurs lines between past and present, private and public, and real and fake, animating the dynamism and dimensionality of Indigenous cultures and identities. ojo|-|ólǫ́ invites collective reflection on the practices of institutions that have accumulated Indigenous art, while advancing a call for Indigenous cultural resurgence in the present and toward our shared future.

Event details

item.title: Moomins' Sea Adventures & Tove and the Sea
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: National Nordic Museum, 2655 NW Market Street, Seattle, WA

Dive into the whimsical world of Moominvalley and the life of its creator, Tove Jansson, with *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* and *Tove and the Sea*. *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* brings Moominvalley to life with illustrations and life-sized characters from the stories. In the center of the exhibit is an interactive island complete with a lighthouse and rowboats that invites visitors to imagine adventures of their own. *Tove and the Sea* offers a more intimate counterpoint, tracing Tove Jansson’s lifelong relationship with the Finnish archipelago through large-scale photographs and reflective texts.

item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: National Nordic Museum, 2655 NW Market Street, Seattle, WA

Dive into the whimsical world of Moominvalley and the life of its creator, Tove Jansson, with *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* and *Tove and the Sea*. *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* brings Moominvalley to life with illustrations and life-sized characters from the stories. In the center of the exhibit is an interactive island complete with a lighthouse and rowboats that invites visitors to imagine adventures of their own. *Tove and the Sea* offers a more intimate counterpoint, tracing Tove Jansson’s lifelong relationship with the Finnish archipelago through large-scale photographs and reflective texts.

Event details

item.title: Figure/Ground: New Criteria
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Henry Art Gallery, 15th Ave NE & 41st St, Seattle, WA

Figure/Ground: New Criteria is inspired by cultural historian Sarah Lewis’s concept of “groundwork aesthetics,” exploring figure-ground relationships and their connection to social tension. It builds on Lewis’s work, which—set against the expansion of Stand Your Ground laws across the United States since 2005, laws that broadly define rights to use lethal self-defense against perceived threats—describes a range of artistic interventions that reimagine “the literal and figurative meaning of ground.” Primarily featuring works from the Henry collection created in the twenty-first century, Figure/Ground reflects a period in which hard-won civil rights and claims to self-determination have been eroded across the US, disproportionately affecting Black, Brown, LGBTQ\+, and other marginalized communities.

Event details

item.title: Beyond Mysticism: The Modern Northwest
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

image: Drift No. 2, 1936, Malcolm M. Roberts, American, 1913-1990, tempera on board, 25 1/2 x 20 5/8 in., Seattle Art Museum, Eugene Fuller Memorial Collection, 37.103, © Malcolm M. Roberts, photo: Scott Leen. Inspired by the region’s natural beauty and rapidly changing city, this exhibition showcases artists who told the raw story of Seattle in the 20th century. By sharing the Pacific Northwest’s perspective through trending art styles—including Social Realism, Surrealism, and Abstract Expressionism—these artists offered a unique take on Modernist art and challenged the “mystic” label ascribed to their works. Spearheading the movement were local icons Mark Tobey, Kenneth Callahan, Guy Anderson, and Morris Graves—but the range and breadth of Pacific Northwest art goes beyond the “Big Four” of the Northwest School. Featuring more than 150 pieces, this exhibition showcases works that investigate the complexities of daily life in the region. Whether applying an ecocritical lens or producing dreamy and uncanny visuals, these artists expressed the duality of the natural world and city life to warn of the environmental and social impacts of industrialization. From Malcolm Roberts to Kenjiro Nomura, learn how Northwest artists applied their regional perspective to broader artistic developments to create a new form of American Modernism. The exhibition is accompanied by a fully illustrated catalogue authored by Theresa Papanikolas, SAM’s Ann M. Barwick Curator of American Art.

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

item.title: Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light invites you to become part of the art. Agha, a Pakistani American artist, animates spaces with her large-scale sculptural installations. Suspended from the ceiling, Agha’s steel cubes are laser-cut with intricate designs that project geometric shadows onto the visitor. She draws on both the light and dark of her own life, using South Asian art practices to convey the gender discrimination she faced growing up as a young girl in Pakistan. An in-depth exploration of Islamic architecture, art, and identity, this is the first solo exhibition of a Pakistani American artist in SAM’s 90-year history. Agha is carving a name out for herself in the art world—visit the Seattle Asian Art Museum for an immersive and illuminating experience.

Event details

item.title: Kameelah Janan Rasheed: we leak, we exceed
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Henry Art Gallery, 15th Ave NE & 41st St, Seattle, WA

Kameelah Janan Rasheed \(b. 1985, East Palo Alto, CA; lives and works in Brooklyn, NY\) is a self-described “learner” who creates multi-media works that explore the poetics-pleasures-politics of Black knowledge production, information technologies, \[un\]learning, and belief formation. She often integrates found images and words alongside her own writing to create collaged wall installations and experimental video works. Across her work, Rasheed investigates the revision and life cycles of text, embracing expanded and embodied forms of writing and reading. we leak, we exceed will activate the unique volume and multiple vantage points of the Henry’s double-height gallery, drawing together threads from physics, Black critical thought, and information theory to create an immersive environment that interrogates the spatial and social implications of compression. A common process used in data storage, spatial organization, and information systems, compression abbreviates and collapses complex ideas into more simplified forms. Rasheed questions the way compression comes at the cost of nuance and creates unrecoverable losses. She draws parallels between the compression of information and the containment of people, both physically and through the structuring and defining of identities. Through a network of video, sound, and architectural mark-making, Rasheed proposes alternatively what she calls “an embrace of Black excess and expansion” as a liberatory practice.

item.title: Ragamala Indian Classical Music and Arts Celebration
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Olympic Sculpture Park, 2901 Western Ave, Seattle, WA

Join Ragamala Seattle for a family-friendly day of Indian music and art activities at the Seattle Asian Art Museum. 11 am-noon Enjoy the beats and rhythms of Indian classical music by participating in a drum circle led by Ravi Albright of the Seattle Tabla Institute. noon-1 pm Learn to sing Hindustani vocal music in this beginner-friendly workshop led by teaching artists Suchitra Iyer and Anindita Das 2-4 pm Enjoy a concert and performance from celebrated Sitar player Pt. Kushal Das accompanied by Shri. Sandip Ghosh on Tabla. Pt. Kushal Das, renowned Sitar and Surbahar artist, was born in a musical family in Kolkata. His father Sailen Das was a disciple of Lakshman Bhattacharya and Pt. Ravi Shankar. His uncle Santanu Das was a disciple of Pt. Ali Akbar Khan. He started his training under the guidance of his father and uncle. Later he received training from Sanjoy Bandopadhyay and from Ajoy Sinha Roy \(a disciple of Pt. Allauddin Khan\) and took vocal lessons from Pt. Manas Chakraborty and Pt. Ramkrishna Basu. Pt. Das will be accompanied on the tabla by Sandip Ghosh, who has been a disciple of Pt. Anindo Chatterjee for the last 18 years. Sandip has accompanied distinguished artists like Pt. Ajoy Chakrabarty, Ud. Shahid Parvez, Ud. Shujaat Khan, Pt. Hari Prasad Chaurasia and Vid. Kaushiki Chakrabarty. He is also a solo artist, collaborating with bands worldwide.

item.title: You've Had It In You All Along
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Avery Barnes Gallery, 120 First Ave. S., Seattle, WA

Artists: Barbara Earl Thomas | Tariqa Waters | Cristina Martinez | Robert Wade | Maikoiyo Alley-Barnes | Nahom Ghirmay “ You’ve Had It In You All Along” is an affirmation of our capability to weather any storm. It serves as a vital reminder that the tools required to reach your destination are already etched within your spirit. We already carry the brilliance and the beauty of what it takes—the confidence, the strength, the security, the poise, and the knowledge to achieve our goals. The grit, the rigor, the radical ideas, and the voice required to transcend the world around us are not external gifts to be acquired; they are inherent powers waiting for the moment of their activation.  In the process of overcoming—when the pathway forward is unclear and the effort feels endless—it is easy to lose sight of our own internal compass. When we stand face-to-face with rejection, heartbreak, or the heavy weight of difficult seasons, this exhibition serves as a sanctuary to remind us that we have always been more than enough, exactly as we are. Breakthroughs happen in the space where we are gentle with ourselves, allowing for the activation of an inner wisdom and intuition that has been present since our first breath.

item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Monday, April 20, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

item.title: Bartender's Circle Summit 2026 - Seattle Cocktail Week
item.description:
Monday, April 20, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Pacific Place Mall, 600 Pine St., Seattle, WA

The world’s bar community is converging on Seattle. For three days, we are transforming the iconic Pacific Place into a multi-floor global campus for the hospitality elite. This isn’t just a regional seminar—it’s where the industry meets to define the next era of cocktail culture.Whether you are traveling from across the ocean or across the city, the Summit offers a masterclass in professional evolution, led by the most influential voices in the game.️ Learn From the LegendsWe’ve curated an unparalleled roster of international talent. Join these icons for deep-dives into the craft:Ivy Mix: Global bar owner and co-founder of Speed Rack.Jeffrey Morgenthaler: Renowned author and master of modern bar management.Camper English: The world’s leading "cocktail scientist" and expert on spirits physics.Jim Romdall: Seattle’s industry veteran on sustainability and long-term bar business....plus dozens more global ambassadors and master distillers. Three Tracks to MasteryOur curriculum is strategically divided into three pillars of excellence, allowing you to customize your education based on your career goals:The Skills Track: Precision meets innovation. These hands-on labs and technical sessions focus on the "how" behind world-class drinks—covering everything from advanced ice programs and molecular science to creative menu development and speed of service.The Spirits Track: This is for the true student of the glass. Go deep into the technical heritage of global categories like Cognac and Pisco, explore distillation secrets with master producers, and refine your palate with curated tastings of rare expressions and new releases.The Business Track: Build a career that lasts. These sessions tackle the "why" and "how much" of the industry—focusing on bar operations, sustainable business models, hospitality leadership, and maintaining physical and mental wellness for a long-term career.️ Secure Your Global AccessThe Summit Class Pass - $45The International Professional’s Choice Full 3-day access to all educational tracks \(Skills, Spirits, Business\) at Pacific Place. Includes entry to exclusive industry lounges and global networking sessions.The Pro-Bundle \(Summit \+ Carnival of Cocktails\) - $75The Ultimate Experience | Best Value Maximize your trip to Seattle. This combo deal is for those who want the full educational foundation plus the high-energy celebration of the Saturday Carnival.Full 3-Day Summit Access: Every class, every speaker, every track.Saturday Carnival Entry: The PNW’s largest spirits tasting event at Pacific Place.12 Tasting Tokens: Sample 100\+ brands and immersive pop-ups.The Bundle Discount: Get the best industry rate by pairing your education and your celebration.

item.title: Bosanko Public Lecture on Sustainability and Growth in Developing Economies
item.description:
Monday, April 20, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Northwest Worklofts (Commons), 107 West Denny Way, Seattle, WA

Seattle University’s Albers School of Business and Economics invites the public to a Bosanko Lecture featuring Aparna Sawhney, Professor at the Centre for the Study of the World Economy in the School of International Studies at Jawaharlal Nehru University \(India\). In this academic public lecture, Professor Sawhney will examine India’s clean energy transition through the lens of sustainability, economic growth, and energy security in a developing-economy context. As one of the world’s fastest-growing economies and home to nearly 1.5 billion people, India faces the complex challenge of expanding energy access while reducing carbon emissions and promoting sustainable development. The lecture will explore India’s renewable energy policies, its experience with tradeable clean energy credits, and emerging approaches to carbon pricing within its broader development strategy. Attendees will gain insight into how policy design, market mechanisms, and international trade intersect with climate goals in developing countries. The lecture will be followed by a moderated Q&A session. This event is open to students, faculty, researchers, policy professionals, and members of the public interested in climate policy, sustainable development, and global energy transitions.

Learn how to handle a knife with confidence, prepare proteins, and turn fresh vegetables and hearty starches into flavorful dishes. Explore the secrets behind rich stocks and silky sauces, as well as the magic of hydrocolloids \(for thickening and gelling\).  Discover how to balance flavors with finesse, work efficiently with smart kitchen mechanics, and keep things safe and clean with proper sanitation. Come sharpen your skills, boost your confidence, and enjoy the satisfaction of creating meals that truly shine. Learning Objectives Cooking methods and skills Knife skills  Working with proteins, vegetables and starches Preparing stocks and sauces, and using hydrocolloids Balancing flavors Kitchen mechanics, sanitation and safety  Who Might Enjoy This Class? Home cooks who would like to cook better, diversify what they make, get better results, and be more efficient with their time in the kitchen  Anyone ready to learn cooking fundamentals and improve their skills  Competent cooks and professionals wanting to fill in gaps in their skills and learn from a pro

item.title: Paella Pop-Up
item.description:
Monday, April 20, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Capitol Cider, 818 E Pike St, Seattle, WA

You know us \(or maybe you don't\). We are obsessed with Northern Mediterranean cuisine. What is more quintessential than Paella when it comes to Northern Mediterranean? \(Well, a few other dishes but paella is certainly one of them\). So, join us for a very limited pop-up for a seafood paella pop-up at a/stir. The ONLY option for dinner is, you guessed it, paella. What is in it? This one will feature smoked chicken thighs, mussels, shrimp and chorizo \(no, we won't forget the saffron, onion and the bell peppers\). No vegan or vegetarian options this time around.  What else comes with it? A glass of Spanish wine - white or red, your choice.  Anything else? Well, if you so choose, you will be able to order a side salad and a basque cheesecake as dessert \(neither are included in price, and both in very limited quantities\).  Seatings at 6pm, 7pm or 8pm. Choose carefully. :-\) Price? $50/person, inclusive of taxes and gratuity. Includes a generous portion of paella and a 5 oz glass of delicious Spanish wine. Salad and Basque cheesecake optionally available at the restaurant at time of dinner. FINE PRINT Since this is a pop-up, we cannot make any accommodations and substitutions with food content. There will NOT be any other regular menu items on the day of service - this is a pop-up for PAELLA only. Not a drinker? Don't wanna pay for the wine? Consider joining us at another pop-up event. Since this is such a limited and narrow scope engagement, we are not in a position to accommodate requests.

item.title: Smarty Pints! Plants, Pollinators, and Cannabis Chemistry
item.description:
Monday, April 20, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Burke-Gilman Brewing Company, 3626 NE 45th St, Suite 102, Seattle, WA

Spring has sprung, so we’re bringing you pollinators and weed\(s\) for Smarty Pints this month! On April 20 we’re back with more timely talks, on how changing snow conditions affect plants and their pollinators and on the chemistry of cannabis, beer, and essential oils. See you at 6:30 in the taproom! 🐝Prof. Berry Brosi Professor, UW Biology Executive Director, MeadoWatch Climate-driven plant-pollinator timing mismatches: does biodiversity provide backup? Pollination is essential for plant reproduction, biodiversity, and agriculture. Scientists are increasingly worried about timing mismatches between plants and their pollinators, driven by climate change. Plants and insects use different environmental cues \(temperature, day length, snowmelt\) to time when they bloom and when they become active, and climate change is increasingly causing these cues to fall out of sync. Most research on this problem has focused on individual plants in isolation, which tend to show negative effects from mistimed pollination. But in nature, most plants are visited by multiple pollinator species and vice versa—so could that biodiversity act as a buffer against these mismatches? To find out, we conducted a study in the Colorado Rockies, where snowmelt largely controls when plants bloom. We experimentally manipulated snowmelt in large plots containing multiple plant species and many individuals of each. Come to the talk to find out what we discovered about how pollinators responded—and whether the plant pollination suffered! 🧪 Jim Roe Analytical Chemist, SoRSE Technology Volatile compounds in cannabis, essential oils, and beer What’s the chemistry behind your favorite smokes, scents, and suds? We’ll discuss terpenes and other -enes, -ones, and -ols that you definitely did not forget after that o-chem final back in college. Jim Roe has made a career out of chemistry instrumentation and analysis, from a research scientist managing the UW Chemistry department’s instrument lab to a field service engineer for equipment spanning the alphabet \(GCMS, HPLC, FTIR… holler for your favorite\). He has consulted on setting up cannabis laboratories around the Pacific Northwest, and currently works in research and development for water-soluble emulsions of cannabinoids, terpenes, hop oils, and other compounds used in food, beverages, and topicals.

Event details

item.title: Free Throw
item.description:
Monday, April 20, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: The Showbox, 1426 1st Ave., Seattle, WA

Since forming in 2012, FREE THROW's story has been simple: keep going. New records, longer tours, bigger rooms - it's always been another step upward for the Nashville-based quintet, whose 2014 debut, Those Days Are Gone, has become an era-defining classic in the emo-punk genre, launching them onto global tours with Hot Mulligan and New Found Glory and slots at Slam Dunk, Riot Fest, and The Fest. Every milestone has pushed them ahead, but on their sixth LP, Moments Before The Wind \(Wax Bodega\), that constant charge gradually slows, suspended mid-step, mid-thought, mid-life. Recorded with longtime producer Brett Romnes on either end of the sold-out Those Days Are Gone 10-year tour, the follow-up to 2023's Lessons That We Swear To Keep chronicles a surrealist descent into liminality: a period of intense personal upheaval colored by loss, renewal, reflection, and, ultimately, life-altering joy through the impending birth of a child. The 11-song set is disarmingly real, colored by sonic spontaneity - a swirl of Midwest emo, sweat-soaked punk fervor, and crashing alternative rock - but also the sort of achingly vulnerable lyricism fans and critics have come to expect from the band. Through it all, Moments finds Free Throw still moving, still growing, but now, perhaps more than ever, acutely aware that the path ahead is neither straight nor obvious. There's a quiet confidence guiding them there into a brand-new era, ready for whatever the wind blows their way.

Event details

item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

item.title: Bartender's Circle Summit 2026 - Seattle Cocktail Week
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Pacific Place Mall, 600 Pine St., Seattle, WA

The world’s bar community is converging on Seattle. For three days, we are transforming the iconic Pacific Place into a multi-floor global campus for the hospitality elite. This isn’t just a regional seminar—it’s where the industry meets to define the next era of cocktail culture.Whether you are traveling from across the ocean or across the city, the Summit offers a masterclass in professional evolution, led by the most influential voices in the game.️ Learn From the LegendsWe’ve curated an unparalleled roster of international talent. Join these icons for deep-dives into the craft:Ivy Mix: Global bar owner and co-founder of Speed Rack.Jeffrey Morgenthaler: Renowned author and master of modern bar management.Camper English: The world’s leading "cocktail scientist" and expert on spirits physics.Jim Romdall: Seattle’s industry veteran on sustainability and long-term bar business....plus dozens more global ambassadors and master distillers. Three Tracks to MasteryOur curriculum is strategically divided into three pillars of excellence, allowing you to customize your education based on your career goals:The Skills Track: Precision meets innovation. These hands-on labs and technical sessions focus on the "how" behind world-class drinks—covering everything from advanced ice programs and molecular science to creative menu development and speed of service.The Spirits Track: This is for the true student of the glass. Go deep into the technical heritage of global categories like Cognac and Pisco, explore distillation secrets with master producers, and refine your palate with curated tastings of rare expressions and new releases.The Business Track: Build a career that lasts. These sessions tackle the "why" and "how much" of the industry—focusing on bar operations, sustainable business models, hospitality leadership, and maintaining physical and mental wellness for a long-term career.️ Secure Your Global AccessThe Summit Class Pass - $45The International Professional’s Choice Full 3-day access to all educational tracks \(Skills, Spirits, Business\) at Pacific Place. Includes entry to exclusive industry lounges and global networking sessions.The Pro-Bundle \(Summit \+ Carnival of Cocktails\) - $75The Ultimate Experience | Best Value Maximize your trip to Seattle. This combo deal is for those who want the full educational foundation plus the high-energy celebration of the Saturday Carnival.Full 3-Day Summit Access: Every class, every speaker, every track.Saturday Carnival Entry: The PNW’s largest spirits tasting event at Pacific Place.12 Tasting Tokens: Sample 100\+ brands and immersive pop-ups.The Bundle Discount: Get the best industry rate by pairing your education and your celebration.

item.title: Moomins' Sea Adventures & Tove and the Sea
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: National Nordic Museum, 2655 NW Market Street, Seattle, WA

Dive into the whimsical world of Moominvalley and the life of its creator, Tove Jansson, with *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* and *Tove and the Sea*. *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* brings Moominvalley to life with illustrations and life-sized characters from the stories. In the center of the exhibit is an interactive island complete with a lighthouse and rowboats that invites visitors to imagine adventures of their own. *Tove and the Sea* offers a more intimate counterpoint, tracing Tove Jansson’s lifelong relationship with the Finnish archipelago through large-scale photographs and reflective texts.

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Book-It Repertory Theatre, 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, WA

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session\\\*\*\* Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate \(paper, t-shirts, etc\) - Water based ink Vera \*does not\* allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. \*\*FAQ\*\* 1. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

item.title: China and India: Conflict and Cooperation in the (De)Globalization Era
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Northwest Worklofts (Commons), 107 West Denny Way, Seattle, WA

The 2026 Peter L. Lee Endowed Lecture in East Asian Culture and Civilization will be given by T.V. Paul, Distinguished James McGill Professor of the Political Science Department at McGill University. 2026 Peter L. Lee Endowed Lecture in East Asian Culture and Civilization Speaker: T.V. Paul Location: Casey Commons 4:00 pm Reception 4:30-6:00 pm Lecture Free and open to Students, Faculty and Community Members Please register to RSVP if interested China and India: Conflict and Cooperation in the \(De\)Globalization Era The relations between Asian giants China and India have been characterized by a mixture of conflict and cooperation. Since the 1962 territorial dispute, there have been intermittent flare-ups with no clear end in sight. However, India and China seem to agree on many issues such as trade, global financial reforms and climate change. This talk explores the multifaceted causes of the peculiar contours of this “managed rivalry.” A broad range of topics such as territory dispute, status, resources, conceptions of regional and world order, strategic culture, balance of power and deterrence strategies will be analyzed. The mitigating role of asymmetrical economic interdependence adopted by the two states as well as their common membership in institutions such as BRICS, SCO and G20 have helped periodic crises from escalating into wars. The China-India relationship is likely to shape 21st century international politics and the Indo-Pacific regional order profoundly.

Event details

item.title: An Evening with Amy Cordalis
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Sinegal Center for Science and Innovation, 200, Seattle, WA

Join us for an evening with Amy Bowers Cordalis, a Yurok mother, fisherwoman, attorney, and nationally recognized leader in Indigenous environmental advocacy. Amy has served as General Counsel of the Yurok Nation and now leads the Ridges to Riffles Indigenous Conservation Group, where she works to advance sovereignty and protect cultural and natural resources, including the undamming of the Klamath River. Her leadership has earned global recognition, including the United Nations Champions of the World Laureate and a place on the 2024 TIME100 Climate List honoring the most influential voices in climate action. She is also the author of The Water Remembers \(Hachette, 2025\) and will have copies of the book available for purchase at the event. Open to the public at no cost, this event offers an opportunity to engage with a powerful voice for land, water, and Indigenous futures. Early registration is encouraged, as space and tickets are limited. Co-sponsored with the Institute for Catholic Thought and Culture and the Center for Environmental Justice and Sustainability.

item.title: Type Design Basics
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Class Overview So you want to design a typeface? Learn how to hand-draw letters and digitize them to create your own unique functional font! In this practical workshop, students will create an original text typeface based on traditional European calligraphy. Whether you want to develop your typography skills, improve your lettering practice, or become a type designer, learning the fundamentals of type design can bring your skills to the next level. Explore both analog media \(pen on paper\) and digital media \(computer\). Learn how to use a calligraphy pen to develop an understanding of the underlying structure of the Latin alphabet. Then you can work on improving your drawings digitally, and create a typeface using Glyphs \(or the font editor of your choice\).   Class time is dedicated to working on your project with individualized feedback, supplemented by lectures, technical demonstrations, and group critique. By the end of the class, you will have a working text typeface of your own creation, as well as a sketch-to-screen workflow that you can use for future font creation. Students will be encouraged to spend up to 10 hours on homework outside of class time. Designing a typeface is a laborious process, but time flies when experimenting, drawing, and editing! This class is offered in partnership with the amazing Seattle Creative Academy \(but this class is not part of their program and is not for credit/degree\)! See also: Experimental Lettering and other Graphic Design or art classes on our website. Learning Objectives Learn the basics of calligraphy Understand the structure and patterning of the Latin alphabet Create your own original letterforms Digitize and refine your original design Learn how to optimize a typeface for text Become familiar with a sketch-to-screen digital type design workflow Who Might Enjoy This Class? Anyone who wants to develop their own custom font Graphic designers, illustrators, lettering artists, type designers -- anyone with related experience who would like to hone their type design skills Architects, engineers, artists, crafters, writers, book lovers -- anyone interested in the form of letters who would enjoy creating and using their own handmade font No prerequisites, but basic graphic design/typography knowledge is recommended

item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Wednesday, April 22, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

Location: Pacific Place Mall, 600 Pine St., Seattle, WA

The world’s bar community is converging on Seattle. For three days, we are transforming the iconic Pacific Place into a multi-floor global campus for the hospitality elite. This isn’t just a regional seminar—it’s where the industry meets to define the next era of cocktail culture.Whether you are traveling from across the ocean or across the city, the Summit offers a masterclass in professional evolution, led by the most influential voices in the game.️ Learn From the LegendsWe’ve curated an unparalleled roster of international talent. Join these icons for deep-dives into the craft:Ivy Mix: Global bar owner and co-founder of Speed Rack.Jeffrey Morgenthaler: Renowned author and master of modern bar management.Camper English: The world’s leading "cocktail scientist" and expert on spirits physics.Jim Romdall: Seattle’s industry veteran on sustainability and long-term bar business....plus dozens more global ambassadors and master distillers. Three Tracks to MasteryOur curriculum is strategically divided into three pillars of excellence, allowing you to customize your education based on your career goals:The Skills Track: Precision meets innovation. These hands-on labs and technical sessions focus on the "how" behind world-class drinks—covering everything from advanced ice programs and molecular science to creative menu development and speed of service.The Spirits Track: This is for the true student of the glass. Go deep into the technical heritage of global categories like Cognac and Pisco, explore distillation secrets with master producers, and refine your palate with curated tastings of rare expressions and new releases.The Business Track: Build a career that lasts. These sessions tackle the "why" and "how much" of the industry—focusing on bar operations, sustainable business models, hospitality leadership, and maintaining physical and mental wellness for a long-term career.️ Secure Your Global AccessThe Summit Class Pass - $45The International Professional’s Choice Full 3-day access to all educational tracks \(Skills, Spirits, Business\) at Pacific Place. Includes entry to exclusive industry lounges and global networking sessions.The Pro-Bundle \(Summit \+ Carnival of Cocktails\) - $75The Ultimate Experience | Best Value Maximize your trip to Seattle. This combo deal is for those who want the full educational foundation plus the high-energy celebration of the Saturday Carnival.Full 3-Day Summit Access: Every class, every speaker, every track.Saturday Carnival Entry: The PNW’s largest spirits tasting event at Pacific Place.12 Tasting Tokens: Sample 100\+ brands and immersive pop-ups.The Bundle Discount: Get the best industry rate by pairing your education and your celebration.

item.description:

Dive into the whimsical world of Moominvalley and the life of its creator, Tove Jansson, with *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* and *Tove and the Sea*. *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* brings Moominvalley to life with illustrations and life-sized characters from the stories. In the center of the exhibit is an interactive island complete with a lighthouse and rowboats that invites visitors to imagine adventures of their own. *Tove and the Sea* offers a more intimate counterpoint, tracing Tove Jansson’s lifelong relationship with the Finnish archipelago through large-scale photographs and reflective texts.

item.title: Beyond Mysticism: The Modern Northwest
item.description:
Wednesday, April 22, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

image: Drift No. 2, 1936, Malcolm M. Roberts, American, 1913-1990, tempera on board, 25 1/2 x 20 5/8 in., Seattle Art Museum, Eugene Fuller Memorial Collection, 37.103, © Malcolm M. Roberts, photo: Scott Leen. Inspired by the region’s natural beauty and rapidly changing city, this exhibition showcases artists who told the raw story of Seattle in the 20th century. By sharing the Pacific Northwest’s perspective through trending art styles—including Social Realism, Surrealism, and Abstract Expressionism—these artists offered a unique take on Modernist art and challenged the “mystic” label ascribed to their works. Spearheading the movement were local icons Mark Tobey, Kenneth Callahan, Guy Anderson, and Morris Graves—but the range and breadth of Pacific Northwest art goes beyond the “Big Four” of the Northwest School. Featuring more than 150 pieces, this exhibition showcases works that investigate the complexities of daily life in the region. Whether applying an ecocritical lens or producing dreamy and uncanny visuals, these artists expressed the duality of the natural world and city life to warn of the environmental and social impacts of industrialization. From Malcolm Roberts to Kenjiro Nomura, learn how Northwest artists applied their regional perspective to broader artistic developments to create a new form of American Modernism. The exhibition is accompanied by a fully illustrated catalogue authored by Theresa Papanikolas, SAM’s Ann M. Barwick Curator of American Art.

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Wednesday, April 22, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

Event details

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Wednesday, April 22, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

item.title: Earth Day Forest Bathing in the Garden
item.description:
Wednesday, April 22, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Taught by Deborah Wilk. Celebrate Earth Day with a mindful Forest Bathing walk in the vibrant spring garden. Slow down, breathe deep, and open your senses with wonder as you deepen your connection to nature. Step away from the stressors of the outside world, as we restore mind, body and spirit while honoring our interdependence with the living world that surrounds and holds us, fostering a sense of gratitude and stewardship for the planet we call Home. Deborah Wilk, psychotherapist and co-founder of Mindful in the Wild, will guide you in practices that integrate mindfulness meditation and shinrin-yoku \(Japanese Forest Bathing\). The walk is approximately one mile and will be on trails with various surfaces, including some gentle inclines down to the more forested areas of the garden.

item.title: You've Had It In You All Along
item.description:
Wednesday, April 22, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Avery Barnes Gallery, 120 First Ave. S., Seattle, WA

Artists: Barbara Earl Thomas | Tariqa Waters | Cristina Martinez | Robert Wade | Maikoiyo Alley-Barnes | Nahom Ghirmay “ You’ve Had It In You All Along” is an affirmation of our capability to weather any storm. It serves as a vital reminder that the tools required to reach your destination are already etched within your spirit. We already carry the brilliance and the beauty of what it takes—the confidence, the strength, the security, the poise, and the knowledge to achieve our goals. The grit, the rigor, the radical ideas, and the voice required to transcend the world around us are not external gifts to be acquired; they are inherent powers waiting for the moment of their activation.  In the process of overcoming—when the pathway forward is unclear and the effort feels endless—it is easy to lose sight of our own internal compass. When we stand face-to-face with rejection, heartbreak, or the heavy weight of difficult seasons, this exhibition serves as a sanctuary to remind us that we have always been more than enough, exactly as we are. Breakthroughs happen in the space where we are gentle with ourselves, allowing for the activation of an inner wisdom and intuition that has been present since our first breath.

item.title: Family Business Exchange | Seminar: Seattle Chocolate (now Maeve)
item.description:
Wednesday, April 22, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Casey Building, 530, Seattle, WA

The Family Business Exchange welcomes Jean Thompson, CEO and Owner of Seattle Chocolate Company, and her daughter, Ellie Thompson, now the second generation in the business, for a fireside chat to share their unique journey together. We'll hear Jean's reflections on her unexpected path into entrepreneurship and how her leadership sparked Ellie’s interest in joining the company. Together, they have navigated family-business dynamics and embraced bold decisions—most notably Ellie’s leadership as Director of Marketing in the recent rebranding to “Maeve.” Hear candid insights from both perspectives on lessons learned, strategies for fostering leadership growth, and a vision for the future that blends legacy with innovation—takeaways every family business leader can apply. About the Family Business Exchange Seattle University’s Family Business Exchange \(FBX\) is a community of Pacific Northwest family business owners who gather to engage in education, networking, and professional development tailored specifically to their needs. FBX provides opportunities for families to address their unique challenges through relevant programming. It serves as a forum for information exchange with the goal of helping each member thrive through future generations. Areas of best practice that we cover include family business succession planning, governance, leadership development and growth, among others. About Equinox Business Law Group Equinox Business Law Group provides strategic legal counsel for entrepreneurs at a fixed monthly cost. Founded by Michelle Bomberger, Equinox brings two decades of legal expertise to entrepreneurs. As business owners themselves, they combine legal knowledge with a powerful network of industry experts to support your business growth. Equinox is a proud Corporate Member of Seattle University’s Family Business Exchange.

item.title: Eric-Paul Riege: ojo|-|ólǫ́
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Henry Art Gallery, 15th Ave NE & 41st St, Seattle, WA

ojo|-|ólǫ́ is an exhibition of recent and newly commissioned work by Diné artist Eric-Paul Riege \(b. 1994, Na’nízhoozhí \[Gallup, New Mexico\]\) that includes sculpture, textile, collage, and video, activated by moments of performance. Across this work, Riege combines customary Diné practices of weaving, silversmithing, and beading with contemporary cultural forms, exploring Diné cosmology, the history of Euro-American trading posts in and adjacent to the Navajo Nation, and the notion of “authenticity” as a value marker of Indigenous art and craft. Developed in partnership between the Henry and The Bell Gallery at Brown University, ojo|-|ólǫ́ emerged from Riege’s material research with Navajo collections housed at Brown’s Haffenreffer Museum of Anthropology and the University of Washington’s Burke Museum of Natural History and Culture. The resulting body of work celebrates the ancestral knowledge and labor contained within Indigenous-made objects, while investigating the role of museums and other institutions in the dissemination and dispossession of knowledge about Indigenous cultures. For his tactile and modular soft sculptures, Riege employs exaggerated scale and combines synthetic and natural materials to playfully question ideas of authenticity and Indigenous artistic production. Processes of making and remaking embody a living practice of exchange that draws together webs of connection across time and space. The exhibition also includes objects from the Burke collection displayed alongside assorted ephemera from Riege’s own home and studio. The resulting non-hierarchal display blurs lines between past and present, private and public, and real and fake, animating the dynamism and dimensionality of Indigenous cultures and identities. ojo|-|ólǫ́ invites collective reflection on the practices of institutions that have accumulated Indigenous art, while advancing a call for Indigenous cultural resurgence in the present and toward our shared future.

Event details

item.title: Figure/Ground: New Criteria
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Henry Art Gallery, 15th Ave NE & 41st St, Seattle, WA

Figure/Ground: New Criteria is inspired by cultural historian Sarah Lewis’s concept of “groundwork aesthetics,” exploring figure-ground relationships and their connection to social tension. It builds on Lewis’s work, which—set against the expansion of Stand Your Ground laws across the United States since 2005, laws that broadly define rights to use lethal self-defense against perceived threats—describes a range of artistic interventions that reimagine “the literal and figurative meaning of ground.” Primarily featuring works from the Henry collection created in the twenty-first century, Figure/Ground reflects a period in which hard-won civil rights and claims to self-determination have been eroded across the US, disproportionately affecting Black, Brown, LGBTQ\+, and other marginalized communities.

Event details

item.title: Beyond Mysticism: The Modern Northwest
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

image: Drift No. 2, 1936, Malcolm M. Roberts, American, 1913-1990, tempera on board, 25 1/2 x 20 5/8 in., Seattle Art Museum, Eugene Fuller Memorial Collection, 37.103, © Malcolm M. Roberts, photo: Scott Leen. Inspired by the region’s natural beauty and rapidly changing city, this exhibition showcases artists who told the raw story of Seattle in the 20th century. By sharing the Pacific Northwest’s perspective through trending art styles—including Social Realism, Surrealism, and Abstract Expressionism—these artists offered a unique take on Modernist art and challenged the “mystic” label ascribed to their works. Spearheading the movement were local icons Mark Tobey, Kenneth Callahan, Guy Anderson, and Morris Graves—but the range and breadth of Pacific Northwest art goes beyond the “Big Four” of the Northwest School. Featuring more than 150 pieces, this exhibition showcases works that investigate the complexities of daily life in the region. Whether applying an ecocritical lens or producing dreamy and uncanny visuals, these artists expressed the duality of the natural world and city life to warn of the environmental and social impacts of industrialization. From Malcolm Roberts to Kenjiro Nomura, learn how Northwest artists applied their regional perspective to broader artistic developments to create a new form of American Modernism. The exhibition is accompanied by a fully illustrated catalogue authored by Theresa Papanikolas, SAM’s Ann M. Barwick Curator of American Art.

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

Event details

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

Event details

item.title: Kameelah Janan Rasheed: we leak, we exceed
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Henry Art Gallery, 15th Ave NE & 41st St, Seattle, WA

Kameelah Janan Rasheed \(b. 1985, East Palo Alto, CA; lives and works in Brooklyn, NY\) is a self-described “learner” who creates multi-media works that explore the poetics-pleasures-politics of Black knowledge production, information technologies, \[un\]learning, and belief formation. She often integrates found images and words alongside her own writing to create collaged wall installations and experimental video works. Across her work, Rasheed investigates the revision and life cycles of text, embracing expanded and embodied forms of writing and reading. we leak, we exceed will activate the unique volume and multiple vantage points of the Henry’s double-height gallery, drawing together threads from physics, Black critical thought, and information theory to create an immersive environment that interrogates the spatial and social implications of compression. A common process used in data storage, spatial organization, and information systems, compression abbreviates and collapses complex ideas into more simplified forms. Rasheed questions the way compression comes at the cost of nuance and creates unrecoverable losses. She draws parallels between the compression of information and the containment of people, both physically and through the structuring and defining of identities. Through a network of video, sound, and architectural mark-making, Rasheed proposes alternatively what she calls “an embrace of Black excess and expansion” as a liberatory practice.

Event details

item.title: You've Had It In You All Along
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Avery Barnes Gallery, 120 First Ave. S., Seattle, WA

Artists: Barbara Earl Thomas | Tariqa Waters | Cristina Martinez | Robert Wade | Maikoiyo Alley-Barnes | Nahom Ghirmay “ You’ve Had It In You All Along” is an affirmation of our capability to weather any storm. It serves as a vital reminder that the tools required to reach your destination are already etched within your spirit. We already carry the brilliance and the beauty of what it takes—the confidence, the strength, the security, the poise, and the knowledge to achieve our goals. The grit, the rigor, the radical ideas, and the voice required to transcend the world around us are not external gifts to be acquired; they are inherent powers waiting for the moment of their activation.  In the process of overcoming—when the pathway forward is unclear and the effort feels endless—it is easy to lose sight of our own internal compass. When we stand face-to-face with rejection, heartbreak, or the heavy weight of difficult seasons, this exhibition serves as a sanctuary to remind us that we have always been more than enough, exactly as we are. Breakthroughs happen in the space where we are gentle with ourselves, allowing for the activation of an inner wisdom and intuition that has been present since our first breath.

item.title: College Possible Washington Coffee Chat
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: SCERA, 625 S. 4th Street, Renton, WA

Coffee Chat with College Possible Washington Join College Possible Washington for a casual Coffee Chat with Executive Director Lauren Treacy. This informal gathering is an opportunity for local partners, supporters, and community members to connect and learn more about our work supporting students on their path to a college degree. College Possible Washington provides intensive college access and success programming for students from low-income backgrounds and those who are the first in their families to attend college. Through personalized coaching and proven support from high school through college graduation, we help students overcome barriers and achieve their educational goals. We’ll share updates on our new full-time staff model, our three-year strategic plan, and the impact of our programs across the greater Seattle area. Attendees will also have the chance to ask questions and engage in conversation about the organization’s direction and community partnerships.
